attack_chain.tool: Cobalt Strike (or Metasploit) with SMB Beacon

attack_chain.architecture_bypass: start-job -RunAs32 (Wow64 cradle for 32-bit shellcode on 64-bit OS)
attack_chain.persistence: Multiple PowerShell processes kept alive in Session 0

shellcode_analysis.type: Cobalt Strike SMB Beacon (reflective DLL injection)
execution_flow: 6. Shellcode copied to allocated memory and executed
7. Shellcode connects to named pipe \\.\ pipe\diagsvc-22
8. SMB beacon operational for lateral pivot
shellcode_analysis.shellcode_hash_note: Shellcode extracted from [Byte[]]$var_code base64 in memory. Contains x86 native code with named pipe C2.
shellcode_analysis.citation: [TOOL: strings extraction from base-wkstn-05-memory.img, pattern: func_get_delegate_type + VirtualAlloc + var_code base64]

findings.description: Cobalt Strike SMB Beacon deployed via WMI lateral movement. Three WMI-spawned PowerShell execution chains created 2018-08-31 01:14-01:31 UTC. Reflective DLL injection via VirtualAlloc shellcode communicating over named pipe \\.\ pipe\diagsvc-22.
findings.citation: [TOOL: volatility3.psscan + strings extraction, evidence: base-wkstn-05-memory.img, PIDs: 2676→4328→1124, shellcode: VirtualAlloc+diagsvc-22]

findings.description: PowerShell PID 1332 used as post-exploitation platform. Spawned 5 rundll32.exe instances between 01:31-21:07 on Aug 31 for additional code injection or task execution.
findings.citation: [TOOL: volatility3.psscan, evidence: base-wkstn-05-memory.img, PPID: 1332, child rundll32 PIDs: 5300, 5056, 4240, 1972, 3720]

findings.description: Orphaned rundll32.exe (PID 7100) running in Session 0 with parent PID 7148 not found. Created 2018-08-31 18:43:50. Still active at capture time with 5 threads and 337 handles — likely a persistent beacon or injected process.
Attacker operated in presence of McAfee AV and Sysmon with Cobalt Strike detection rules. The reflective injection and named-pipe C2 likely evaded signature-based detection.
findings.citation: [INFERENCE: McAfee processes running (psscan), Sysmon config with CS detection rules (strings), yet attack succeeded. Confidence: MEDIUM]
